title: RedBlackTree Insertion Operations
content:
setWithHintNode: Insert/update using a hint node to speed up nearby insertions.

close to the expected insertion position (often the previously returned node in a loop).

When the hint is a good fit (sorted / nearly-sorted insertion), this can avoid most of the
normal root-to-leaf search and reduce constant factors.

When the hint does not match (random workloads), this will fall back to the normal set path. (O(log n)). setWithHint: Boolean wrapper for setWithHintNode. (O(log n)). set: Insert or update a key/value (map mode) or key-only (set mode).

This method is optimized for:
- monotonic inserts via min/max boundary fast paths
- updates via a single-pass search (no double walk) (O(log n))

title: TreeMultiMap Overview
content:
TreeMultiMap (ordered MultiMap) — key → bucket (Array of values).

Semantics (RFC):
- Bucketed design: each key appears once; duplicates live in the bucket.
- `get(key)` returns a **live** bucket reference.
- Default iteration yields bucket entries: `[K, V[]]`.
- Navigable operations (`first/last/ceiling/...`) return entry tuples like TreeMap.

title: Trie Insertion Operations
content: add: Insert one word into the trie. (O(L)). addMany: Insert many words from an iterable. (O(ΣL))
embeddingText: Trie insertion operations: add: Insert one word into the trie. (O(L)). addMany: Insert many words from an iterable. (O(ΣL))

title: Trie Deletion Operations
content: clear: Remove all words and reset to a fresh root. (O(1)). delete: Delete one word if present. (O(L))
embeddingText: Trie deletion operations: clear: Remove all words and reset to a fresh root. (O(1)). delete: Delete one word if present. (O(L))

title: Trie Lookup Operations
content: has: Check whether a word exists. (O(L)). getHeight: Compute the height (max depth) of the trie. (O(N)). hasPurePrefix: Check whether input is a proper prefix of at least one word. (O(L)). hasPrefix: Check whether any word starts with input. (O(L)). hasCommonPrefix: Check whether the trie’s longest common prefix equals input. (O(min(H,L)). getLongestCommonPrefix: Return the longest common prefix among all words. (O(H)). getWords: Collect words under a prefix up to a maximum count. (O(K·L))
